crime.value: Murder by stabbing of Star Tucker and killing of unnamed man who fell down stairs
crime.quote: Rufus found Star dead in the bedroom from a stab wound

victim.value: Star Tucker, wife of Blake who had hidden gambling winnings that dangerous people wanted back
victim.quote: The guys Blake won the money from wanted their money back, even though Blake won it fair and square

closed_world.value: Southern Arms Hotel and surrounding neighborhood including Pinky's bar
closed_world.quote: Star and Blake lived on the third floor of the Southern Arms Hotel. Pinky's bar was just around the corner

culprit_and_motive.value: Unnamed man killed Star to get the money Blake had hidden, but died accidentally fleeing the scene
culprit_and_motive.quote: A man came out and limped to the stairway to go down... he fell and was killed instantly

resolution.value: No formal resolution - Rufus takes money as payment and lets Blake escape with remaining cash
resolution.quote: Blake pulled out 5,000 dollars and laid the bag on the ground, Blake turned and walked away

investigator.value: Rufus Hancock, retired police detective turned private investigator hired by Star Tucker
investigator.quote: Rufus was a detective for twenty one years for the city. Now retired, Rufus worked as a Private Investigator

suspect_architecture.value: Limited suspect pool: Blake Tucker (missing husband, gambling debts), unnamed crippled man (letter courier, dies accidentally), Star Tucker (victim). Motives center on gambling winnings and debt collection rather than traditional means/motive/opportunity spread.
suspect_architecture.quote: The guys Blake won the money from wanted their money back, even though Blake won it fair and square.

red_herring.value: Blake's disappearance initially presented as mysterious vanishing, misdirecting from the real issue of gambling debts and hiding from creditors.
red_herring.quote: Star said her husband had never been gone this long. The last time Star saw Blake he was writing a letter.

clue_fairness.value: The bloody letter's contents are revealed to the reader when Rufus reads it, providing the same information about the hidden money and secret location.
clue_fairness.quote: It read there is 10,000 dollars in our secret place. I may be already dead when you get this in the mail

social_world.value: Working-class milieu of hotel residents, bar regulars, and gambling debts. Social hierarchy between retired detective turned PI and his clients.
social_world.quote: Star and Blake lived on the third floor of the Southern Arms Hotel. Pinky's bar was just around the corner.

alibi.value: Blake's alibi of being missing/possibly dead is false - he was hiding from gambling creditors while planning to retrieve money and flee.
alibi.quote: Blake had been hiding out until he could get home to get the money.

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical discovery of hidden safe behind medicine cabinet, opened using Blake's mother's name as combination, revealing the gambling winnings.
reveal_mechanic.quote: when he tried Blake's mother, SNAP went a sound and Rufus easily pulled the door open.

investigator_wound.value: Rufus's corruption/greed - he decides to steal the money rather than pursue justice, rationalizing it as payment for his work.
investigator_wound.quote: Rufus convinced himself that the money would not be missed and was payment for the work he had done

_meta.title: ebook daniel books the bloody letter secret
